evals.prompt: Add a primary PIE button that submits a form in React
evals.expected_output: React import from pie-webc/react/button.js, PieButton with variant='primary' and type='submit', docs link.
expectations: Response includes: import { PieButton } from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/button.js'
Response shows <PieButton variant="primary" type="submit">
Response does NOT hardcode hex color values
Response includes a link to https://pie.design/components/button/

evals.prompt: Build a React login form with an email text input, password input, and a submit button using PIE components
evals.expected_output: React imports for PieTextInput and PieButton, correct import paths from pie-webc/react/*, usage with type='email' and type='password', form structure.
expectations: Response imports PieTextInput from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/text-input.js'
Response imports PieButton from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/button.js'
Response uses type='email' on the email input and type='password' on the password input
Response wraps components in a <form> element
Response does NOT use bare pie-* custom element tags (it's a React project)

evals.prompt: How do I show a form validation error on a PIE text input in React? The email field should turn red and show 'Invalid email address'
evals.expected_output: PieTextInput with status='error', PieAssistiveText with variant='error' below the input, correct React imports for both components.
expectations: Response imports PieAssistiveText from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/assistive-text.js'
Response shows status='error' on PieTextInput
Response shows PieAssistiveText with variant='error' containing the error message
Response does NOT suggest setting a red border color manually via CSS

evals.prompt: I need a React modal with a title, some body text, a confirm button and a cancel button using PIE
evals.expected_output: PieModal import from pie-webc/react/modal.js, usage with heading prop, PieButton variants for confirm (primary) and cancel (secondary or ghost), docs link.
expectations: Response imports PieModal from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/modal.js'
Response shows the heading prop or slot on PieModal
Response uses a primary or destructive PieButton for the confirm action
Response uses a secondary, ghost or outline PieButton for cancel
Response includes a link to https://pie.design/components/modal/

evals.prompt: Add a React loading spinner while data is being fetched from an API
evals.expected_output: PieSpinner import from pie-webc/react/spinner.js, conditional render based on loading state, mention of brand or default variant, docs link.
expectations: Response imports PieSpinner from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/spinner.js'
Response shows conditional rendering (e.g. {isLoading && <PieSpinner />})
Response does NOT recommend showing multiple spinners
Response includes a link to https://pie.design/components/spinner/

evals.prompt: I want to show a React notification banner after a failed payment — it should be dismissible and show an error
evals.expected_output: PieNotification with variant='error', isDismissible prop, React import from pie-webc/react/notification.js, explanation that notifications persist until dismissed for errors.
expectations: Response imports PieNotification from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/notification.js'
Response uses variant='error' on PieNotification
Response includes isDismissible prop or notes the component supports dismissal
Response explains that error notifications should persist (not auto-dismiss)

evals.prompt: How do I implement a React checkbox group for dietary preferences (Vegetarian, Vegan, Gluten-free)?
evals.expected_output: PieCheckboxGroup with PieCheckbox children, React imports for both, name/value props, controlled state with onChange.
expectations: Response imports PieCheckbox from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/checkbox.js'
Response imports PieCheckboxGroup from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/checkbox-group.js'
Response nests PieCheckbox components inside PieCheckboxGroup
Response shows value and name props on each PieCheckbox
Response does NOT import only PieCheckbox without PieCheckboxGroup

evals.prompt: Build a React card component that is clickable and navigates to a restaurant page when clicked
evals.expected_output: PieCard with interactive mode/prop, React import from pie-webc/react/card.js, onClick handler or href, mention of the interactive variant.
expectations: Response imports PieCard from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/card.js'
Response mentions the interactive prop or variant to make the card clickable
Response shows an onClick handler or href attribute
Response does NOT wrap a non-interactive card in a plain <div onClick>
Response includes a link to https://pie.design/components/card/

evals.prompt: I need a React icon button for a close/dismiss action in a header. No visible label.
evals.expected_output: PieIconButton import from pie-webc/react/icon-button.js, aria-label prop for accessibility, icon slot or prop, note that aria-label is required.
expectations: Response imports PieIconButton from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/icon-button.js'
Response includes aria-label prop on PieIconButton
Response explains that aria-label is required because there is no visible text label
Response does NOT use a regular PieButton with hidden text as the primary recommendation

evals.prompt: How do I add PIE tabs in React to switch between 'Overview', 'Reviews', and 'Menu' sections?
evals.expected_output: PieTabs React import, tab items/panels structure, controlled or uncontrolled selection, docs link.
expectations: Response imports PieTabs from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/tabs.js'
Response shows a structure with tab items for Overview, Reviews, and Menu
Response includes a link to https://pie.design/components/tabs/

evals.prompt: We need to show a destructive confirmation in React when the user clicks 'Delete account'. Which PIE component and how?
evals.expected_output: PieModal with a destructive PieButton variant for confirm and a secondary/ghost for cancel, explanation that modal is correct for irreversible actions.
expectations: Response recommends PieModal for a destructive confirmation
Response uses variant='destructive' on the confirm PieButton
Response uses a secondary, ghost, or outline PieButton for cancel
Response explains why modal is appropriate (blocks background, forces decision)
Response does NOT recommend toast or notification for a destructive confirmation

evals.prompt: I'm setting up a new React + Vite project and want to use PIE. Walk me through the setup.
evals.expected_output: npm install @justeattakeaway/pie-webc, import @justeattakeaway/pie-css in main entry, React wrapper import pattern, no special Vite config needed.
expectations: Response includes: npm install @justeattakeaway/pie-webc
Response shows importing @justeattakeaway/pie-css in the app entry point
Response shows the React wrapper import pattern from pie-webc/react/*.js
Response does NOT require complex build config (React+Vite needs none)
Response does NOT show bare web component imports as the primary pattern for React

evals.prompt: What's the correct way to style a custom component using PIE color tokens in React? I need brand background with white text.
evals.expected_output: CSS custom properties --dt-color-interactive-brand for background and --dt-color-content-inverse for text, used in a className or style object, explanation of alias token semantics.
expectations: Response uses --dt-color-interactive-brand for the brand background
Response uses --dt-color-content-inverse for text on dark/brand backgrounds
Response uses CSS var() syntax
Response does NOT hardcode hex values like #FF8000 or #FFFFFF
Response explains that alias tokens adapt to light/dark themes

evals.prompt: How do I add a React select dropdown for choosing a delivery time slot with PIE?
evals.expected_output: PieSelect import from pie-webc/react/select.js, option elements inside, label association, docs link.
expectations: Response imports PieSelect from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/select.js'
Response shows <option> elements or equivalent inside PieSelect
Response includes a label or PieFormLabel association
Response includes a link to https://pie.design/components/select/

evals.prompt: I need a React toggle switch for enabling/disabling push notifications in a settings page
evals.expected_output: PieSwitch import from pie-webc/react/switch.js, checked/onChange controlled pattern, label association.
expectations: Response imports PieSwitch from '@justeattakeaway/pie-webc/react/switch.js'
Response shows controlled usage with checked prop and onChange handler
Response associates a visible label with the switch
Response does NOT use a checkbox as the recommended component for a toggle

evals.prompt: Should I use a modal or a side sheet for a React filter panel on desktop that the user keeps open while browsing results?
evals.expected_output: Side sheet recommended because it keeps main content visible, modal blocks background interaction. Modal reserved for focused decisions.
expectations: Response recommends side sheet for a filter panel the user keeps open while browsing
Response explains that side sheet keeps the main content partially visible
Response explains that modal blocks background interaction (wrong for this use case)
Response imports or references PieSideSheet
Response does NOT recommend modal as correct for this desktop browsing scenario

evals.prompt: Add a React skeleton loader for a restaurant listing page that's loading
evals.expected_output: PieSkeleton component, explanation that skeleton is better than spinner for full page/list loading because content shape is known, import path.
expectations: Response recommends PieSkeleton over PieSpinner for a full listing page
Response explains why skeleton is preferred (known content shape, better perceived performance)
Response imports or references the pie-skeleton component
Response does NOT recommend spinner as the primary solution for a full page loading state

evals.prompt: What's the PIE spacing token equivalent of margin-bottom: 24px in React inline styles or a CSS class?
evals.expected_output: Identify --dt-spacing-e as 24px, show CSS var syntax, and show how to use in React inline styles or className.
expectations: Response identifies --dt-spacing-e as the 24px spacing token
Response shows var(--dt-spacing-e) syntax
Response shows usage in either a CSS class or React inline style object
Response does NOT suggest hardcoding 24px

evals.prompt: I need to add pagination to a React restaurant search results page using PIE
evals.expected_output: Should check whether PiePagination exists in pie-webc (it may not be released yet). If it doesn't exist, should recommend fetching live docs and offer an accessible fallback using PieButton + nav element. Should NOT confidently provide a fake import path for a component that doesn't exist.
expectations: Response either correctly imports PiePagination if it exists, OR acknowledges it may not be available and directs to live docs at https://pie.design/components/pagination/
Response does NOT fabricate an import path for a component without verifying it exists
Response provides a working path forward (either the real component or a PIE-token-styled accessible fallback)
Response includes a link to https://pie.design/components/pagination/

evals.prompt: How do I add icons to a PIE button in React — e.g. a basket icon on an 'Add to basket' button?
evals.expected_output: Separate @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-webc package with React import from /dist/react/IconBasket.js, placed as a slot/child inside PieButton, note that icons are a separate package and pie-icons-react is deprecated.
expectations: Response mentions @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-webc as the icon package
Response shows: npm install @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-webc
Response shows a React import from '@justeattakeaway/pie-icons-webc/dist/react/IconBasket.js' or similar
Response shows placing the icon component inside or alongside PieButton
Response does NOT suggest @justeattakeaway/pie-webc provides icons
Response does NOT suggest using an tag for the icon
Response does NOT recommend the deprecated @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-react package

evals.prompt: I'm using @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-react in my React app for PIE icons. Is that correct?
evals.expected_output: Should warn that @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-react is deprecated and recommend migrating to @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-webc, showing the React-specific import path from /dist/react/.
expectations: Response warns that @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-react is deprecated
Response recommends @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-webc as the replacement
Response shows a React import from '@justeattakeaway/pie-icons-webc/dist/react/IconName.js' or similar
Response shows: npm install @justeattakeaway/pie-icons-webc
Response does NOT tell the user their current setup is correct
